title: v2 invoke-accepted response (deferred completion protocol)
description: Response body a plugin returns synchronously for a contract_version 2 deferred action invocation, acknowledging that the request was accepted for asynchronous processing. Only the terminal outcome is unknown at this point; the plugin already knows (and MUST report) whether the action requires a browser continuation (interaction_mode). This document intentionally embeds interaction_mode in the response itself, rather than requiring a reader to cross-reference the plugin manifest, so that the launch_url requirement is enforceable from this schema alone.

result_mode.description: How the eventual result will be delivered once the operation reaches a terminal state. Declared up front so hosts can prepare result handling without waiting for completion.

interaction_mode.description: none: no browser handoff is required for this operation. launch_url: the end user must continue in a browser context reachable at launch_url (present on this same response).

launch_url.description: Present if and only if interaction_mode is launch_url. A locator only: the browser continuation itself is authenticated separately via the interaction-assertion JWT (see docs/security.md); this URL carries no ambient authority on its own.
